3 affected subjects unmapped changes present
  • specled.mix_tasks clean
  • specled.package clean
  • specled.spec_review clean

Affected subjects (3)

Mix tasks for scaffolding, session-start priming, indexing, guiding, validating, summarizing, and strictly checking Spec Led Development workspaces.

specled.mix_tasks .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md SPEC EDITED
Changes in this PR: 1 added

Requirements (13)

  • specled.tasks.init_scaffold must stable

    mix spec.init shall create the canonical .spec/ workspace with README.md, AGENTS.md, decisions/README.md, spec_system.spec.md, and package.spec.md.

  • specled.tasks.init_local_skill should evolving

    In interactive runs, mix spec.init shall offer to scaffold a local Skill for Spec Led Development and write it when the prompt is accepted.

  • specled.tasks.decision_new_scaffold should evolving

    mix spec.decision.new shall scaffold an ADR under .spec/decisions with the required frontmatter and body sections.

  • specled.tasks.index_writes_state must stable

    mix spec.index shall build the authored subject and decision index and write .spec/state.json.

  • specled.tasks.prime_context should evolving

    mix spec.prime shall provide a read-only session-start summary that combines workspace status, current-branch guidance, and the default local loop without writing current-truth files or derived state.

  • specled.tasks.next_guidance should evolving

    mix spec.next shall provide a read-only guided reconciliation step that points at the next subject, proof, or ADR update for the current Git change set and tell the maintainer when the branch is ready for the full local check.

  • specled.tasks.prime_json should evolving

    mix spec.prime shall support JSON output for agent consumption and shall only execute eligible command verifications when --run-commands is passed.

  • specled.tasks.validate_findings must stable

    mix spec.validate shall validate specs, derive findings, and write .spec/state.json with a verification report before returning.

  • specled.tasks.validate_exit_status must stable

    mix spec.validate shall exit non-zero whenever the generated verification report status is fail.

  • specled.tasks.check_strict_gate must stable

    mix spec.check shall run indexing, strict validation, and branch-aware co-change enforcement, failing on any errors or warnings.

  • specled.tasks.status_summary should evolving

    mix spec.status shall summarize coverage, verification strength, weak spots, and ADR usage for the current workspace, executing command verifications by default unless explicitly opted out.

  • specled.tasks.no_app_start must stable

    No mix spec.* task shall call Mix.Task.run("app.start") or otherwise require the host OTP application to be running, since spec tasks perform only file I/O, Git CLI calls, and in-memory parsing.

  • NEW specled.tasks.review_html_artifact should evolving

    mix spec.review shall produce a self-contained HTML artifact rendering the current Git change set as a spec-aware PR review surface, delegating the substantive contract to specled.spec_review.

Files (1)
  • lib/
    • mix/
      • tasks/
        • spec.review.ex
lib/mix/tasks/spec.review.ex
@@ -0,0 +1,100 @@
1+defmodule Mix.Tasks.Spec.Review do
2+ use Mix.Task
3+
4+ @shortdoc "Renders a spec-aware PR review HTML artifact for the current change set"
5+
6+ # covers: specled.tasks.review_html_artifact
7+ # covers: specled.spec_review.diff_against_base
8+ # covers: specled.spec_review.same_artifact_local_and_ci
9+
10+ @moduledoc """
11+ Builds a self-contained HTML artifact rendering the current Git change
12+ set as a spec-aware PR review surface.
13+
14+ ## Options
15+
16+ * `--base` — explicit base ref. Defaults to the same auto-detection
17+ as `mix spec.next` (origin/main, main, master, HEAD).
18+ * `--output` — output file path. Defaults to `_build/spec_review.html`.
19+ * `--root` — repo root. Defaults to the current working directory.
20+ * `--open` — open the rendered file in the default browser when running
21+ locally. No-op in CI / when `open`/`xdg-open` are unavailable.
22+
23+ The same code path produces the same artifact locally and in CI. The
24+ HTML embeds all CSS and JavaScript inline; no network resources are
25+ fetched at view time.
26+ """
27+
28+ @impl Mix.Task
29+ def run(args) do
30+ {opts, rest, invalid} =
31+ OptionParser.parse(
32+ args,
33+ strict: [
34+ root: :string,
35+ spec_dir: :string,
36+ base: :string,
37+ output: :string,
38+ open: :boolean
39+ ],
40+ aliases: [r: :root, o: :output]
41+ )
42+
43+ validate_args!(rest, invalid)
44+
45+ root = opts[:root] || File.cwd!()
46+ spec_dir = opts[:spec_dir] || SpecLedEx.detect_spec_dir(root)
47+ authored_dir = SpecLedEx.detect_authored_dir(root, spec_dir)
48+ output_path = output_path(opts[:output], root)
49+
50+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root, spec_dir: spec_dir, authored_dir: authored_dir)
51+ view = SpecLedEx.Review.build_view(index, root, base: opts[:base])
52+ html = SpecLedEx.Review.Html.render(view) |> IO.iodata_to_binary()
53+
54+ File.mkdir_p!(Path.dirname(output_path))
55+ File.write!(output_path, html)
56+
57+ Mix.shell().info(
58+ "spec.review wrote #{Path.relative_to(output_path, root)} (#{byte_size(html)} bytes)"
59+ )
60+
61+ Mix.shell().info(
62+ " base=#{view.meta.base_ref} head=#{view.meta.head_ref} affected_subjects=#{length(view.affected_subjects)} findings=#{length(view.all_findings)}"
63+ )
64+
65+ if opts[:open], do: maybe_open(output_path)
66+
67+ :ok
68+ end
69+
70+ defp output_path(nil, root), do: Path.join([root, "_build", "spec_review.html"])
71+
72+ defp output_path(path, root) do
73+ if Path.type(path) == :absolute, do: path, else: Path.expand(path, root)
74+ end
75+
76+ defp maybe_open(path) do
77+ binary =
78+ case :os.type() do
79+ {:unix, :darwin} -> "open"
80+ {:unix, _} -> "xdg-open"
81+ _ -> nil
82+ end
83+
84+ if binary && System.find_executable(binary) do
85+ _ = System.cmd(binary, [path])
86+ :ok
87+ else
88+ :ok
89+ end
90+ end
91+
92+ defp validate_args!([], []), do: :ok
93+
94+ defp validate_args!(rest, invalid) do
95+ invalid_flags = Enum.map(invalid, fn {flag, _value} -> flag end)
96+ extra_args = Enum.map(rest, &inspect/1)
97+ details = Enum.join(invalid_flags ++ extra_args, ", ")
98+ Mix.raise("Invalid arguments for spec.review: #{details}")
99+ end
100+end
ℹ How is requirement coverage computed?

Each requirement is verified by one or more spec-verification entries that name it in their covers: list. The verifier inspects each entry and reports the strongest evidence it could find for the requirement.

EXECUTED
A kind: command entry with execute: true ran and exited 0; or a kind: tagged_tests entry executed its matching test successfully. Requires mix spec.check --run-commands (or mix spec.review --run-commands).
LINKED
For a file-kind verification (source_file, test_file, guide_file, …) the target file exists and its content references the requirement id (typically a # covers: comment or a literal mention). For tagged_tests, a test in the suite carries @tag spec: "<requirement_id>".
CLAIMED
The verification entry names the requirement in its covers: list. A textual claim only — nothing else has been checked yet.
UNCOVERED
No verification entry covers this requirement at all. Add one to spec-verification.

Tightening the floor. Pass --min-strength linked (or executed) to mix spec.check to require every claim reach that tier or fail. Set verification_minimum_strength: linked in a subject's spec-meta to bake that floor into the spec itself.

Priority (must / should / may) is the requirement author's RFC-2119 intent. Stability (stable / evolving) communicates whether the wording is settled or still being tightened. Hover any chip on this page for a one-line definition.

Requirement coverage (13)

ADRs referenced (4)

specled.decision.declarative_current_truth Declarative Current Truth With Git History accepted2026-03-11

Context

Spec Led Development needs a durable current-state contract without turning .spec/ into a second project tracker.

Decision

Keep .spec/specs/*.spec.md as current truth only. Use Git branches, commits, and pull requests as the time dimension for change history. Store only durable cross-cutting ADRs under .spec/decisions/*.md.

Consequences

The workspace stays declarative. Agents and humans update current truth directly, use ADRs for stable policy, and rely on mix spec.check plus Git review to catch missing co-changes.

.spec/decisions/specled.decision.declarative_current_truth.md
specled.decision.local_skill_scaffold Local Skill Scaffold Supports Repo Usage accepted2026-03-11

Context

Initialized repositories need local guidance that helps agents maintain .spec consistently without hard-coding package-specific behavior into the main tool.

Decision

mix spec.init scaffolds a thin local Skill for Spec Led Development when the interactive prompt is accepted. The local Skill explains repo usage, when to update current-truth subjects, when to add an ADR, and when mix spec.check --base ... should fail.

Consequences

Repositories get project-local guidance without changing the core format. The upstream package keeps the reusable workflow, while local Skills carry repository-specific conventions.

.spec/decisions/specled.decision.local_skill_scaffold.md
specled.decision.guided_reconciliation_loop Guided Reconciliation Before Strict Enforcement accepted2026-03-20

Context

Spec Led Development needs a gentler local loop for small changes, brownfield work, and regression fixes, especially when a maintainer is still learning the workspace.

Strict drift enforcement alone catches missing co-changes, but it does not tell the maintainer what to do next.

Decision

Add a read-only guided reconciliation step before strict enforcement.

Use mix spec.prime at session start to combine workspace health, current-branch guidance, and the default local loop in one read-only output.

Use mix spec.next to inspect the current Git change set, point at the impacted subject or uncovered frontier, and suggest the next spec, proof, or ADR action.

Keep .spec authored and deterministic.

Keep branch guarding inside mix spec.check and use mix spec.status for frontier reporting, but do not let those commands auto-edit current truth.

Consequences

The default local loop becomes: orient with mix spec.prime when entering a branch, make the change, tighten the proof, run mix spec.next, update current truth, then run mix spec.check --base ....

Brownfield adoption becomes easier because uncovered frontier files are reported explicitly instead of being left implicit.

Branch-local planning notes such as docs/plans/*.md are not treated as current-truth policy surfaces.

.spec/decisions/specled.decision.guided_reconciliation_loop.md
specled.decision.no_app_start Spec Tasks Shall Not Start the OTP Application accepted2026-04-07

Context

All mix spec.* tasks called Mix.Task.run("app.start") at the top of their run/1 function. This caused problems when spec tooling was used inside host applications with slow startup, required environment variables, or side-effectful boot sequences. Spec checks became slow, could incorrectly fail due to missing host configuration, and produced noisy output unrelated to spec validation.

Decision

Remove Mix.Task.run("app.start") from every mix spec.* task. Spec tasks perform only file I/O, Git CLI calls, and in-memory parsing — none of which require the host OTP application to be running.

Consequences

Spec tasks run faster and no longer couple to host application boot. They can be invoked in CI or development without satisfying the host app’s runtime prerequisites.

.spec/decisions/specled.decision.no_app_start.md

Elixir package for Spec Led Development. Provides Mix tasks to scaffold, orient, index, guide, validate, summarize, and strictly check authored specs.

specled.package .spec/specs/package.spec.md SPEC EDITED
Changes in this PR: 1 modified

Requirements (4)

  • MODIFIED specled.package.mix_tasks must stable

    The package shall provide mix spec.init, mix spec.prime, mix spec.next, mix spec.check, mix spec.status, mix spec.decision.new, mix spec.index, mix spec.validate, and mix spec.review as user-facing commands.

  • specled.package.default_local_loop should evolving

    The package README shall teach mix spec.prime as the session-start context command, a default local loop centered on mix spec.next and mix spec.check, explain the ready-for-check decision, reserve ADRs for durable cross-cutting policy, and present mix spec.status as occasional plus mix spec.index and mix spec.validate as advanced plumbing.

  • specled.package.index_and_state must stable

    The package shall index authored subject specs, index durable ADRs, and write derived state to .spec/state.json.

  • specled.package.declarative_governance must stable

    The package shall keep `.spec` declarative and current-state only, using ADRs for durable cross-cutting policy and Git history for change over time.

Files (2)
  • README.md
  • mix.exs
README.md
@@ -42,6 +42,12 @@ These are helpful, but they are not part of the default local loop:
4242- `mix spec.decision.new`
4343 - scaffolds a durable ADR under `.spec/decisions/`
4444 - use it only when the change is durable and cross-cutting
45+- `mix spec.review`
46+ - renders the current Git change set as a self-contained HTML PR review surface
47+ - spec-first navigation: each affected subject is a card with the prose statement headline + tabs for Spec / Code / Coverage / Decisions
48+ - top triage panel for findings (collapses to a single confirmation row when clean), inline finding badges per subject, and a "outside the spec system" panel for unmapped changes
49+ - same artifact runs locally and in CI; defaults to `_build/spec_review.html`
50+ - see [CI Integration](#ci-integration) for the GitHub Actions + GitHub Pages workflow
4551
4652## Advanced Commands
4753
@@ -162,3 +168,25 @@ Use `.spec/decisions/*.md` only for durable cross-cutting ADRs. Do not add in-fl
162168
163169GitHub Actions runs the same command through [`scripts/check_specs.sh`](scripts/check_specs.sh)
164170when `.spec/`, library code, or Mix configuration changes.
171+
172+## CI Integration
173+
174+`mix spec.review` is designed to render the same artifact in CI that you see locally. A copy-pastable GitHub Actions workflow is shipped in this package at:
175+
176+```
177+priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex
178+```
179+
180+To adopt:
181+
182+1. Copy the file (it is plain YAML — the `.eex` extension is for consistency with other scaffolded templates) to `.github/workflows/spec-review.yml` in your repository.
183+2. Enable GitHub Pages: Settings → Pages → "Deploy from a branch" → branch `gh-pages`, folder `/ (root)`.
184+3. The default `GITHUB_TOKEN` already has the permissions the workflow declares (`contents: write`, `pull-requests: write`).
185+
186+On every PR open / push / reopen, the workflow:
187+
188+- runs `mix spec.review --base origin/<base_ref> --output spec_review.html`
189+- deploys the rendered HTML to `gh-pages` under `/pr/<PR>/index.html`
190+- posts (or updates, via a hidden marker comment) a PR comment linking to `https://<owner>.github.io/<repo>/pr/<PR>/`
191+
192+The workflow is hand-rolled with plain `git` and `gh` (no third-party Actions beyond `actions/checkout`, `erlef/setup-beam`, and `actions/cache`). Auto-cleanup of stale `/pr/<PR>/` paths after merge is intentionally not included in this version — add a separate cleanup job if your repository has many PRs.
mix.exs
@@ -24,7 +24,8 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.MixProject do
2424 [
2525 {:jason, "~> 1.4"},
2626 {:yaml_elixir, "~> 2.11"},
27− {:zoi, "~> 0.17"}
27+ {:zoi, "~> 0.17"},
28+ {:earmark, "~> 1.4"}
2829 ]
2930 end
3031end
ℹ How is requirement coverage computed?

Each requirement is verified by one or more spec-verification entries that name it in their covers: list. The verifier inspects each entry and reports the strongest evidence it could find for the requirement.

EXECUTED
A kind: command entry with execute: true ran and exited 0; or a kind: tagged_tests entry executed its matching test successfully. Requires mix spec.check --run-commands (or mix spec.review --run-commands).
LINKED
For a file-kind verification (source_file, test_file, guide_file, …) the target file exists and its content references the requirement id (typically a # covers: comment or a literal mention). For tagged_tests, a test in the suite carries @tag spec: "<requirement_id>".
CLAIMED
The verification entry names the requirement in its covers: list. A textual claim only — nothing else has been checked yet.
UNCOVERED
No verification entry covers this requirement at all. Add one to spec-verification.

Tightening the floor. Pass --min-strength linked (or executed) to mix spec.check to require every claim reach that tier or fail. Set verification_minimum_strength: linked in a subject's spec-meta to bake that floor into the spec itself.

Priority (must / should / may) is the requirement author's RFC-2119 intent. Stability (stable / evolving) communicates whether the wording is settled or still being tightened. Hover any chip on this page for a one-line definition.

Requirement coverage (4)

EXECUTED › LINKED › CLAIMED › UNCOVERED strongest evidence wins per requirement

    The package shall provide mix spec.init, mix spec.prime, mix spec.next, mix spec.check, mix spec.status, mix spec.decision.new, mix spec.index, mix spec.validate, and mix spec.review as user-facing commands.

  • specled.package.default_local_loop should LINKED

  • specled.package.index_and_state must CLAIMED

    The package shall index authored subject specs, index durable ADRs, and write derived state to .spec/state.json.

    • command mix test test/specled_ex_test.exs test/specled_ex/index_state_test.exs CLAIMED
  • specled.package.declarative_governance must CLAIMED

    The package shall keep `.spec` declarative and current-state only, using ADRs for durable cross-cutting policy and Git history for change over time.

    • command mix test test/specled_ex_test.exs test/specled_ex/index_state_test.exs CLAIMED
Raw spec-verification block (3 entries)

The author's declarations as written in the spec file. The per-requirement view above is computed from this.

  • command mix test test/specled_ex_test.exs test/specled_ex/index_state_test.exs executes
    specled.package.index_and_state specled.package.declarative_governance
  • readme_file README.md declared
    specled.package.default_local_loop
  • command mix run -e 'Mix.Task.load_all(); Enum.each(~w(spec.init spec.prime spec.next spec.check spec.status spec.decision.new spec.index spec.validate spec.review), fn task -> Mix.Task.get(task) || raise("missing #{task}") end)' executes
    specled.package.mix_tasks

ADRs referenced (4)

specled.decision.declarative_current_truth Declarative Current Truth With Git History accepted2026-03-11

Context

Spec Led Development needs a durable current-state contract without turning .spec/ into a second project tracker.

Decision

Keep .spec/specs/*.spec.md as current truth only. Use Git branches, commits, and pull requests as the time dimension for change history. Store only durable cross-cutting ADRs under .spec/decisions/*.md.

Consequences

The workspace stays declarative. Agents and humans update current truth directly, use ADRs for stable policy, and rely on mix spec.check plus Git review to catch missing co-changes.

.spec/decisions/specled.decision.declarative_current_truth.md
specled.decision.local_skill_scaffold Local Skill Scaffold Supports Repo Usage accepted2026-03-11

Context

Initialized repositories need local guidance that helps agents maintain .spec consistently without hard-coding package-specific behavior into the main tool.

Decision

mix spec.init scaffolds a thin local Skill for Spec Led Development when the interactive prompt is accepted. The local Skill explains repo usage, when to update current-truth subjects, when to add an ADR, and when mix spec.check --base ... should fail.

Consequences

Repositories get project-local guidance without changing the core format. The upstream package keeps the reusable workflow, while local Skills carry repository-specific conventions.

.spec/decisions/specled.decision.local_skill_scaffold.md
specled.decision.explicit_subject_ownership Explicit Subject Ownership For Self-Hosted Internals accepted2026-03-11

Context

Spec Led Development can exercise internal helper modules indirectly through tests and workflows while still leaving those helpers unowned by any authored current-truth subject. That weakens self-hosting because the package source may be verified in practice without being represented clearly in .spec/specs/.

Decision

Keep self-hosted package coverage explicit. Every shipped source module should belong to at least one meaningful current-truth subject, and low-level helpers should be grouped into coherent ownership subjects instead of left as accidental coverage.

Consequences

The self-hosted .spec/specs/ set may grow when the package adds new internal responsibilities. Coverage reports become easier to interpret because uncovered source files indicate real authored gaps rather than indirect test coverage hiding missing subject ownership.

.spec/decisions/specled.decision.explicit_subject_ownership.md
specled.decision.guided_reconciliation_loop Guided Reconciliation Before Strict Enforcement accepted2026-03-20

Context

Spec Led Development needs a gentler local loop for small changes, brownfield work, and regression fixes, especially when a maintainer is still learning the workspace.

Strict drift enforcement alone catches missing co-changes, but it does not tell the maintainer what to do next.

Decision

Add a read-only guided reconciliation step before strict enforcement.

Use mix spec.prime at session start to combine workspace health, current-branch guidance, and the default local loop in one read-only output.

Use mix spec.next to inspect the current Git change set, point at the impacted subject or uncovered frontier, and suggest the next spec, proof, or ADR action.

Keep .spec authored and deterministic.

Keep branch guarding inside mix spec.check and use mix spec.status for frontier reporting, but do not let those commands auto-edit current truth.

Consequences

The default local loop becomes: orient with mix spec.prime when entering a branch, make the change, tighten the proof, run mix spec.next, update current truth, then run mix spec.check --base ....

Brownfield adoption becomes easier because uncovered frontier files are reported explicitly instead of being left implicit.

Branch-local planning notes such as docs/plans/*.md are not treated as current-truth policy surfaces.

.spec/decisions/specled.decision.guided_reconciliation_loop.md

Renders a Git change set as a self-contained HTML PR review surface. Spec-first navigation with prose-headlined subject cards, per-subject tabs (Spec / Code / Coverage / Decisions), top triage panel for findings, and a misc panel for unmapped changes. Same artifact runs locally and in CI.

specled.spec_review .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md NEW SUBJECT
NEW SUBJECT — this spec file did not exist on the base ref.

Requirements (10)

  • NEW specled.spec_review.html_artifact must evolving

    mix spec.review shall produce a self-contained HTML file with all CSS and JavaScript embedded so the artifact renders correctly without any external network resources at view time.

  • NEW specled.spec_review.spec_first_navigation must evolving

    The HTML shall organize content by spec subject as the primary unit, with each affected subject rendered as a card whose headline is the subject's human-readable prose statement rather than its raw YAML or file path.

  • NEW specled.spec_review.per_subject_tabs must evolving

    Each subject card shall expose four tabs - Spec, Code, Coverage, and Decisions - that pivot from the subject's intent to its supporting artifacts. The Code tab shall organize changes by realized_by binding tier rather than by file path.

  • NEW specled.spec_review.triage_panel must evolving

    The HTML shall render a top-of-page triage panel that summarizes findings (count, severity breakdown) and the affected subject count for the change set. The panel shall collapse to a single confirmation row when there are zero findings, so clean change sets feel clean.

  • NEW specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges must evolving

    Findings affecting a subject shall additionally render as inline badges on that subject's card, in addition to appearing in the top-of-page triage panel.

  • NEW specled.spec_review.misc_panel must evolving

    Files that change in the diff but do not map to any spec subject shall be surfaced in a clearly-labeled "outside the spec system" panel as a flat file diff, rather than excluded from the artifact.

  • NEW specled.spec_review.same_artifact_local_and_ci must evolving

    mix spec.review shall produce the same HTML artifact locally and in CI, sourced from the same code path with no local-only or CI-only rendering modes.

  • NEW specled.spec_review.diff_against_base must evolving

    mix spec.review shall accept an explicit --base ref and otherwise default to the same base detection used by mix spec.next (origin/main, main, master, HEAD).

  • NEW specled.spec_review.read_only_viewer must evolving

    The HTML artifact shall be a read-only viewer in v1 with no acceptance state, no per-subject sign-off, and no reviewer-attributed approval recorded by the artifact itself.

  • NEW specled.spec_review.gh_pages_distribution should evolving

    A documented GitHub Actions workflow shall be provided that runs mix spec.review on PR open and synchronize, deploys the rendered HTML to a per-PR path on a GitHub Pages branch, and posts or updates a PR comment containing the link.

Scenarios (6)

  • NEW specled.spec_review.clean_pr_collapses specled.spec_review.triage_panel
    given
    • a PR whose change set produces zero findings
    when
    • mix spec.review renders the HTML artifact
    then
    • the top-of-page triage panel collapses to a single confirmation row
    • no severity counts are surfaced
  • NEW specled.spec_review.finding_renders_top_and_inline specled.spec_review.triage_panel specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges
    given
    • a PR whose change set produces a triangulation finding affecting subject S
    when
    • mix spec.review renders the HTML artifact
    then
    • the top-of-page triage panel lists the finding with its severity
    • subject S's card carries an inline badge for the finding
  • NEW specled.spec_review.unmapped_change_in_misc_panel specled.spec_review.misc_panel
    given
    • a PR that changes a file with no spec subject mapping (e.g., a dependency bump)
    when
    • mix spec.review renders the HTML artifact
    then
    • the file appears in the "outside the spec system" misc panel
    • the misc panel renders the change as a flat file diff
    • the file is not silently excluded from the artifact
  • NEW specled.spec_review.self_contained_html specled.spec_review.html_artifact
    given
    • a rendered spec.review HTML artifact
    when
    • the file is opened in a browser with no network connectivity
    then
    • all CSS, JavaScript, and structural content render correctly
    • the artifact does not request any external network resources
  • NEW specled.spec_review.subject_card_prose_headline specled.spec_review.spec_first_navigation
    given
    • a PR that affects subject S whose statement field is "S protects credentials at the boundary"
    when
    • mix spec.review renders the HTML artifact
    then
    • subject S's card headline is the prose statement, not the YAML id or file path
    • the YAML id is available as supporting metadata, not the primary headline
  • NEW specled.spec_review.code_tab_grouped_by_binding specled.spec_review.per_subject_tabs
    given
    • a PR whose code changes touch MFAs in both the api_boundary and implementation tiers of subject S
    when
    • the reviewer opens subject S's Code tab in the rendered HTML artifact
    then
    • the changes are grouped under their realized_by tier headers
    • file paths appear under each tier rather than as the primary organization

Spec file changes

.spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md
@@ -0,0 +1,179 @@
1+# Spec Review
2+
3+A self-contained HTML artifact that turns a Git change set into a spec-aware PR review surface.
4+
5+## Intent
6+
7+Render the structured triangulation that specled_ex already produces (spec ↔ binding ↔ test ↔ coverage) as a human review surface, so the rigor that today exists only as a CI gate becomes legible during the act of review. Reviewers see intent first (the spec's prose statement), then pivot to code, tests, coverage, and decisions from the same anchor. Changes that don't map to any subject are surfaced honestly in a labeled "outside the spec system" panel rather than hidden.
8+
9+The artifact is read-only in v1. Acceptance still happens via the host platform's existing approval flow (e.g., GitHub's Approve button); the artifact's job is to make that approval informed, not to record it.
10+
11+```spec-meta
12+id: specled.spec_review
13+kind: workflow
14+status: active
15+summary: Renders a Git change set as a self-contained HTML PR review surface. Spec-first navigation with prose-headlined subject cards, per-subject tabs (Spec / Code / Coverage / Decisions), top triage panel for findings, and a misc panel for unmapped changes. Same artifact runs locally and in CI.
16+surface:
17+ - lib/mix/tasks/spec.review.ex
18+ - lib/specled_ex/review.ex
19+ - lib/specled_ex/review/file_diff.ex
20+ - lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex
21+ - lib/specled_ex/review/spec_diff.ex
22+ - priv/spec_review_assets/*
23+ - test/specled_ex/review_test.exs
24+ - test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs
25+decisions:
26+ - specled.decision.spec_review_html_viewer
27+```
28+
29+## Requirements
30+
31+```spec-requirements
32+- id: specled.spec_review.html_artifact
33+ statement: mix spec.review shall produce a self-contained HTML file with all CSS and JavaScript embedded so the artifact renders correctly without any external network resources at view time.
34+ priority: must
35+ stability: evolving
36+- id: specled.spec_review.spec_first_navigation
37+ statement: The HTML shall organize content by spec subject as the primary unit, with each affected subject rendered as a card whose headline is the subject's human-readable prose statement rather than its raw YAML or file path.
38+ priority: must
39+ stability: evolving
40+- id: specled.spec_review.per_subject_tabs
41+ statement: Each subject card shall expose four tabs - Spec, Code, Coverage, and Decisions - that pivot from the subject's intent to its supporting artifacts. The Code tab shall organize changes by realized_by binding tier rather than by file path.
42+ priority: must
43+ stability: evolving
44+- id: specled.spec_review.triage_panel
45+ statement: The HTML shall render a top-of-page triage panel that summarizes findings (count, severity breakdown) and the affected subject count for the change set. The panel shall collapse to a single confirmation row when there are zero findings, so clean change sets feel clean.
46+ priority: must
47+ stability: evolving
48+- id: specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges
49+ statement: Findings affecting a subject shall additionally render as inline badges on that subject's card, in addition to appearing in the top-of-page triage panel.
50+ priority: must
51+ stability: evolving
52+- id: specled.spec_review.misc_panel
53+ statement: Files that change in the diff but do not map to any spec subject shall be surfaced in a clearly-labeled "outside the spec system" panel as a flat file diff, rather than excluded from the artifact.
54+ priority: must
55+ stability: evolving
56+- id: specled.spec_review.same_artifact_local_and_ci
57+ statement: mix spec.review shall produce the same HTML artifact locally and in CI, sourced from the same code path with no local-only or CI-only rendering modes.
58+ priority: must
59+ stability: evolving
60+- id: specled.spec_review.diff_against_base
61+ statement: mix spec.review shall accept an explicit --base ref and otherwise default to the same base detection used by mix spec.next (origin/main, main, master, HEAD).
62+ priority: must
63+ stability: evolving
64+- id: specled.spec_review.read_only_viewer
65+ statement: The HTML artifact shall be a read-only viewer in v1 with no acceptance state, no per-subject sign-off, and no reviewer-attributed approval recorded by the artifact itself.
66+ priority: must
67+ stability: evolving
68+- id: specled.spec_review.gh_pages_distribution
69+ statement: A documented GitHub Actions workflow shall be provided that runs mix spec.review on PR open and synchronize, deploys the rendered HTML to a per-PR path on a GitHub Pages branch, and posts or updates a PR comment containing the link.
70+ priority: should
71+ stability: evolving
72+```
73+
74+## Scenarios
75+
76+```spec-scenarios
77+- id: specled.spec_review.clean_pr_collapses
78+ given:
79+ - a PR whose change set produces zero findings
80+ when:
81+ - mix spec.review renders the HTML artifact
82+ then:
83+ - the top-of-page triage panel collapses to a single confirmation row
84+ - no severity counts are surfaced
85+ covers:
86+ - specled.spec_review.triage_panel
87+- id: specled.spec_review.finding_renders_top_and_inline
88+ given:
89+ - a PR whose change set produces a triangulation finding affecting subject S
90+ when:
91+ - mix spec.review renders the HTML artifact
92+ then:
93+ - the top-of-page triage panel lists the finding with its severity
94+ - subject S's card carries an inline badge for the finding
95+ covers:
96+ - specled.spec_review.triage_panel
97+ - specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges
98+- id: specled.spec_review.unmapped_change_in_misc_panel
99+ given:
100+ - a PR that changes a file with no spec subject mapping (e.g., a dependency bump)
101+ when:
102+ - mix spec.review renders the HTML artifact
103+ then:
104+ - the file appears in the "outside the spec system" misc panel
105+ - the misc panel renders the change as a flat file diff
106+ - the file is not silently excluded from the artifact
107+ covers:
108+ - specled.spec_review.misc_panel
109+- id: specled.spec_review.self_contained_html
110+ given:
111+ - a rendered spec.review HTML artifact
112+ when:
113+ - the file is opened in a browser with no network connectivity
114+ then:
115+ - all CSS, JavaScript, and structural content render correctly
116+ - the artifact does not request any external network resources
117+ covers:
118+ - specled.spec_review.html_artifact
119+- id: specled.spec_review.subject_card_prose_headline
120+ given:
121+ - a PR that affects subject S whose statement field is "S protects credentials at the boundary"
122+ when:
123+ - mix spec.review renders the HTML artifact
124+ then:
125+ - subject S's card headline is the prose statement, not the YAML id or file path
126+ - the YAML id is available as supporting metadata, not the primary headline
127+ covers:
128+ - specled.spec_review.spec_first_navigation
129+- id: specled.spec_review.code_tab_grouped_by_binding
130+ given:
131+ - a PR whose code changes touch MFAs in both the api_boundary and implementation tiers of subject S
132+ when:
133+ - the reviewer opens subject S's Code tab in the rendered HTML artifact
134+ then:
135+ - the changes are grouped under their realized_by tier headers
136+ - file paths appear under each tier rather than as the primary organization
137+ covers:
138+ - specled.spec_review.per_subject_tabs
139+```
140+
141+## Verification
142+
143+```spec-verification
144+- kind: command
145+ target: mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs
146+ execute: true
147+ covers:
148+ - specled.spec_review.html_artifact
149+ - specled.spec_review.spec_first_navigation
150+ - specled.spec_review.per_subject_tabs
151+ - specled.spec_review.triage_panel
152+ - specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges
153+ - specled.spec_review.misc_panel
154+ - specled.spec_review.same_artifact_local_and_ci
155+ - specled.spec_review.diff_against_base
156+ - specled.spec_review.read_only_viewer
157+- kind: source_file
158+ target: lib/mix/tasks/spec.review.ex
159+ covers:
160+ - specled.spec_review.diff_against_base
161+ - specled.spec_review.same_artifact_local_and_ci
162+- kind: source_file
163+ target: lib/specled_ex/review.ex
164+ covers:
165+ - specled.spec_review.spec_first_navigation
166+ - specled.spec_review.misc_panel
167+- kind: source_file
168+ target: lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex
169+ covers:
170+ - specled.spec_review.html_artifact
171+ - specled.spec_review.per_subject_tabs
172+ - specled.spec_review.triage_panel
173+ - specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges
174+ - specled.spec_review.read_only_viewer
175+- kind: workflow_file
176+ target: priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex
177+ covers:
178+ - specled.spec_review.gh_pages_distribution
179+```

lib/mix/tasks/spec.review.ex
@@ -0,0 +1,100 @@
1+defmodule Mix.Tasks.Spec.Review do
2+ use Mix.Task
3+
4+ @shortdoc "Renders a spec-aware PR review HTML artifact for the current change set"
5+
6+ # covers: specled.tasks.review_html_artifact
7+ # covers: specled.spec_review.diff_against_base
8+ # covers: specled.spec_review.same_artifact_local_and_ci
9+
10+ @moduledoc """
11+ Builds a self-contained HTML artifact rendering the current Git change
12+ set as a spec-aware PR review surface.
13+
14+ ## Options
15+
16+ * `--base` — explicit base ref. Defaults to the same auto-detection
17+ as `mix spec.next` (origin/main, main, master, HEAD).
18+ * `--output` — output file path. Defaults to `_build/spec_review.html`.
19+ * `--root` — repo root. Defaults to the current working directory.
20+ * `--open` — open the rendered file in the default browser when running
21+ locally. No-op in CI / when `open`/`xdg-open` are unavailable.
22+
23+ The same code path produces the same artifact locally and in CI. The
24+ HTML embeds all CSS and JavaScript inline; no network resources are
25+ fetched at view time.
26+ """
27+
28+ @impl Mix.Task
29+ def run(args) do
30+ {opts, rest, invalid} =
31+ OptionParser.parse(
32+ args,
33+ strict: [
34+ root: :string,
35+ spec_dir: :string,
36+ base: :string,
37+ output: :string,
38+ open: :boolean
39+ ],
40+ aliases: [r: :root, o: :output]
41+ )
42+
43+ validate_args!(rest, invalid)
44+
45+ root = opts[:root] || File.cwd!()
46+ spec_dir = opts[:spec_dir] || SpecLedEx.detect_spec_dir(root)
47+ authored_dir = SpecLedEx.detect_authored_dir(root, spec_dir)
48+ output_path = output_path(opts[:output], root)
49+
50+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root, spec_dir: spec_dir, authored_dir: authored_dir)
51+ view = SpecLedEx.Review.build_view(index, root, base: opts[:base])
52+ html = SpecLedEx.Review.Html.render(view) |> IO.iodata_to_binary()
53+
54+ File.mkdir_p!(Path.dirname(output_path))
55+ File.write!(output_path, html)
56+
57+ Mix.shell().info(
58+ "spec.review wrote #{Path.relative_to(output_path, root)} (#{byte_size(html)} bytes)"
59+ )
60+
61+ Mix.shell().info(
62+ " base=#{view.meta.base_ref} head=#{view.meta.head_ref} affected_subjects=#{length(view.affected_subjects)} findings=#{length(view.all_findings)}"
63+ )
64+
65+ if opts[:open], do: maybe_open(output_path)
66+
67+ :ok
68+ end
69+
70+ defp output_path(nil, root), do: Path.join([root, "_build", "spec_review.html"])
71+
72+ defp output_path(path, root) do
73+ if Path.type(path) == :absolute, do: path, else: Path.expand(path, root)
74+ end
75+
76+ defp maybe_open(path) do
77+ binary =
78+ case :os.type() do
79+ {:unix, :darwin} -> "open"
80+ {:unix, _} -> "xdg-open"
81+ _ -> nil
82+ end
83+
84+ if binary && System.find_executable(binary) do
85+ _ = System.cmd(binary, [path])
86+ :ok
87+ else
88+ :ok
89+ end
90+ end
91+
92+ defp validate_args!([], []), do: :ok
93+
94+ defp validate_args!(rest, invalid) do
95+ invalid_flags = Enum.map(invalid, fn {flag, _value} -> flag end)
96+ extra_args = Enum.map(rest, &inspect/1)
97+ details = Enum.join(invalid_flags ++ extra_args, ", ")
98+ Mix.raise("Invalid arguments for spec.review: #{details}")
99+ end
100+end
lib/specled_ex/review.ex
@@ -0,0 +1,320 @@
1+defmodule SpecLedEx.Review do
2+ @moduledoc """
3+ Assembles the view-model rendered as the spec-aware PR review HTML.
4+
5+ The renderer (`SpecLedEx.Review.Html`) is intentionally dumb — it expects
6+ the view-model returned by `build_view/3` and does no further data
7+ fetching. All git interaction, finding aggregation, and per-subject
8+ grouping happens here.
9+ """
10+
11+ alias SpecLedEx.{ChangeAnalysis, Coverage, Verifier}
12+ alias SpecLedEx.Review.{FileDiff, SpecDiff}
13+
14+ @doc """
15+ Builds the view-model for the current change set.
16+
17+ Options:
18+ * `:base` — explicit base ref (defaults to ChangeAnalysis detection)
19+ * `:strict`, `:run_commands`, `:command_timeout_ms`, `:min_strength` —
20+ forwarded to `Verifier.verify/3`
21+ """
22+ def build_view(index, root, opts \\ []) do
23+ base_opt = Keyword.get(opts, :base)
24+ analysis = ChangeAnalysis.analyze(index, root, base: base_opt)
25+ verifier_opts = Keyword.drop(opts, [:base])
26+ verifier_report = Verifier.verify(index, root, verifier_opts)
27+
28+ subjects_by_id = subjects_by_id(index)
29+ findings_by_subject = group_findings_by_subject(verifier_report["findings"])
30+
31+ affected_subject_ids =
32+ MapSet.union(
33+ MapSet.new(analysis.changed_subject_ids),
34+ MapSet.new(analysis.impacted_subject_ids)
35+ )
36+ |> MapSet.to_list()
37+ |> Enum.sort()
38+
39+ all_diffs = FileDiff.for_files(root, analysis.base, analysis.changed_files)
40+
41+ claims_by_subject = group_claims_by_subject(verifier_report)
42+
43+ affected_subjects =
44+ Enum.flat_map(affected_subject_ids, fn id ->
45+ case Map.get(subjects_by_id, id) do
46+ nil ->
47+ []
48+
49+ subject ->
50+ [
51+ build_subject_view(
52+ id,
53+ subject,
54+ analysis,
55+ all_diffs,
56+ findings_by_subject,
57+ root,
58+ Map.get(claims_by_subject, id, %{})
59+ )
60+ ]
61+ end
62+ end)
63+
64+ mapped_files = collect_mapped_files(affected_subjects)
65+
66+ # covers: specled.spec_review.misc_panel
67+ # Files in the change set that don't map to any subject are surfaced
68+ # honestly here as flat file diffs rather than dropped from the view.
69+ unmapped_files =
70+ analysis.changed_files
71+ |> Enum.reject(&MapSet.member?(mapped_files, &1))
72+ |> Enum.reject(&policy_file?/1)
73+ |> Enum.sort()
74+
75+ unmapped_changes =
76+ Enum.map(unmapped_files, fn path ->
77+ %{file: path, lines: Map.get(all_diffs, path, [])}
78+ end)
79+
80+ decisions_changed = build_decisions_changed(analysis, index)
81+ findings = verifier_report["findings"] || []
82+ triage = build_triage(findings, affected_subjects, unmapped_changes != [])
83+ adrs_by_id = build_adrs_by_id(index, root, analysis.base)
84+
85+ %{
86+ meta: %{
87+ base_ref: analysis.base,
88+ head_ref: head_ref(root),
89+ generated_at: DateTime.utc_now() |> DateTime.truncate(:second),
90+ verifier_status: verifier_report["status"],
91+ repo_root: root
92+ },
93+ triage: triage,
94+ affected_subjects: affected_subjects,
95+ unmapped_changes: unmapped_changes,
96+ decisions_changed: decisions_changed,
97+ adrs_by_id: adrs_by_id,
98+ all_findings: findings
99+ }
100+ end
101+
102+ defp build_adrs_by_id(index, root, base_ref) do
103+ (index["decisions"] || [])
104+ |> Map.new(fn decision ->
105+ meta = decision["meta"]
106+ id = meta_field(meta, :id, nil)
107+ file = decision["file"]
108+ title = decision["title"] || id
109+
110+ data = %{
111+ id: id,
112+ file: file,
113+ title: title,
114+ status: meta_field(meta, :status, nil),
115+ date: meta_field(meta, :date, nil),
116+ change_type: meta_field(meta, :change_type, nil),
117+ affects: meta_field(meta, :affects, []),
118+ superseded_by: meta_field(meta, :superseded_by, nil),
119+ replaces: meta_field(meta, :replaces, nil),
120+ body_text: read_adr_body(root, file),
121+ change_status: SpecDiff.adr_change_status(root, base_ref, file)
122+ }
123+
124+ {id, data}
125+ end)
126+ end
127+
128+ defp read_adr_body(root, file) when is_binary(file) do
129+ case File.read(Path.join(root, file)) do
130+ {:ok, content} -> strip_frontmatter(content)
131+ _ -> ""
132+ end
133+ end
134+
135+ defp read_adr_body(_root, _file), do: ""
136+
137+ defp strip_frontmatter("---\n" <> rest) do
138+ case String.split(rest, "\n---\n", parts: 2) do
139+ [_frontmatter, body] -> String.trim_leading(body, "\n")
140+ _ -> rest
141+ end
142+ end
143+
144+ defp strip_frontmatter(content), do: content
145+
146+ defp subjects_by_id(index) do
147+ (index["subjects"] || [])
148+ |> Map.new(fn subject -> {Coverage.subject_id(subject), subject} end)
149+ end
150+
151+ # covers: specled.spec_review.spec_first_navigation
152+ # The view-model is keyed on subject id with the prose statement front and
153+ # center; the renderer treats each affected_subject entry as the primary
154+ # navigation unit, never the underlying file paths.
155+ defp build_subject_view(
156+ id,
157+ subject,
158+ analysis,
159+ all_diffs,
160+ findings_by_subject,
161+ root,
162+ claims_by_req
163+ ) do
164+ meta = subject["meta"]
165+ statement = meta_field(meta, :summary, "")
166+ title = subject["title"] || id
167+ file = subject["file"]
168+
169+ subject_files = Map.get(analysis.subject_file_map, id, MapSet.new())
170+
171+ changed_files =
172+ analysis.changed_files
173+ |> Enum.filter(&MapSet.member?(subject_files, &1))
174+ |> Enum.sort()
175+
176+ code_changes =
177+ Enum.map(changed_files, fn path ->
178+ %{file: path, lines: Map.get(all_diffs, path, [])}
179+ end)
180+
181+ spec_file_changed? = is_binary(file) and file in analysis.changed_files
182+
183+ spec_diff =
184+ if spec_file_changed? do
185+ %{file: file, lines: Map.get(all_diffs, file, [])}
186+ else
187+ nil
188+ end
189+
190+ spec_changes =
191+ if spec_file_changed? do
192+ SpecDiff.compute(root, analysis.base, subject)
193+ else
194+ empty_spec_changes()
195+ end
196+
197+ %{
198+ id: id,
199+ file: file,
200+ title: title,
201+ statement: statement,
202+ bindings: meta_field(meta, :realized_by, %{}),
203+ decision_refs: meta_field(meta, :decisions, []),
204+ requirements: subject["requirements"] || [],
205+ scenarios: subject["scenarios"] || [],
206+ verification: subject["verification"] || [],
207+ code_changes: code_changes,
208+ spec_diff: spec_diff,
209+ spec_changes: spec_changes,
210+ findings: Map.get(findings_by_subject, id, []),
211+ claims_by_req: claims_by_req,
212+ changed_files: changed_files
213+ }
214+ end
215+
216+ defp group_claims_by_subject(verifier_report) do
217+ claims =
218+ verifier_report
219+ |> Map.get("verification", %{})
220+ |> Map.get("claims", [])
221+
222+ claims
223+ |> Enum.group_by(& &1["subject_id"])
224+ |> Map.new(fn {sid, sub_claims} ->
225+ grouped = Enum.group_by(sub_claims, & &1["cover_id"])
226+ {sid, grouped}
227+ end)
228+ end
229+
230+ defp empty_spec_changes do
231+ %{
232+ file_changed?: false,
233+ base_existed?: true,
234+ requirements: %{added: [], modified: [], removed: [], unchanged_ids: MapSet.new()},
235+ scenarios: %{added: [], modified: [], removed: [], unchanged_ids: MapSet.new()}
236+ }
237+ end
238+
239+ defp meta_field(nil, _key, default), do: default
240+ defp meta_field(meta, key, default) when is_struct(meta), do: Map.get(meta, key) || default
241+ defp meta_field(meta, key, default) when is_map(meta) do
242+ Map.get(meta, key) || Map.get(meta, Atom.to_string(key)) || default
243+ end
244+
245+ defp collect_mapped_files(affected_subjects) do
246+ affected_subjects
247+ |> Enum.flat_map(& &1.changed_files)
248+ |> MapSet.new()
249+ end
250+
251+ defp policy_file?(path) do
252+ String.starts_with?(path, ".spec/specs/") or String.starts_with?(path, ".spec/decisions/")
253+ end
254+
255+ defp build_triage(findings, affected_subjects, has_unmapped?) do
256+ by_severity =
257+ findings
258+ |> Enum.group_by(& &1["severity"])
259+ |> Map.new(fn {sev, items} -> {sev, length(items)} end)
260+
261+ affected_summaries =
262+ Enum.map(affected_subjects, fn s ->
263+ sev_counts =
264+ s.findings
265+ |> Enum.group_by(& &1["severity"])
266+ |> Map.new(fn {sev, items} -> {sev, length(items)} end)
267+
268+ %{
269+ id: s.id,
270+ findings_count: length(s.findings),
271+ by_severity: sev_counts
272+ }
273+ end)
274+
275+ %{
276+ findings_count: length(findings),
277+ by_severity: by_severity,
278+ affected_subject_count: length(affected_subjects),
279+ affected_subjects: affected_summaries,
280+ has_unmapped_changes?: has_unmapped?,
281+ clean?: findings == [] and not has_unmapped? and affected_subjects == []
282+ }
283+ end
284+
285+ defp group_findings_by_subject(findings) do
286+ findings
287+ |> List.wrap()
288+ |> Enum.group_by(& &1["subject_id"])
289+ end
290+
291+ defp build_decisions_changed(analysis, index) do
292+ decision_files = Enum.filter(analysis.changed_files, &ChangeAnalysis.decision_file?/1)
293+ decisions_by_file = Map.new(index["decisions"] || [], &{&1["file"], &1})
294+
295+ Enum.map(decision_files, fn file ->
296+ case Map.get(decisions_by_file, file) do
297+ nil ->
298+ %{id: nil, file: file, status: nil, change_type: nil, affects: []}
299+
300+ decision ->
301+ meta = decision["meta"] || %{}
302+
303+ %{
304+ id: meta["id"],
305+ file: file,
306+ status: meta["status"],
307+ change_type: meta["change_type"],
308+ affects: meta["affects"] || []
309+ }
310+ end
311+ end)
312+ end
313+
314+ defp head_ref(root) do
315+ case System.cmd("git", ["-C", root, "rev-parse", "--short", "HEAD"], stderr_to_stdout: true) do
316+ {out, 0} -> String.trim(out)
317+ _ -> "HEAD"
318+ end
319+ end
320+end
lib/specled_ex/review/file_diff.ex
@@ -0,0 +1,137 @@
1+defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.FileDiff do
2+ @moduledoc false
3+
4+ @type line_kind :: :file_header | :hunk_header | :add | :del | :ctx
5+ @type line :: {line_kind, String.t()}
6+
7+ @doc """
8+ Returns a map of `path => [line]` for the given paths, computed by
9+ diffing `base` against the working tree (which includes committed and
10+ uncommitted changes). Untracked files are surfaced as full additions.
11+
12+ An empty `paths` list returns `%{}` without invoking git.
13+ """
14+ @spec for_files(String.t(), String.t(), [String.t()]) :: %{String.t() => [line]}
15+ def for_files(_root, _base, []), do: %{}
16+
17+ def for_files(root, base, paths) when is_list(paths) do
18+ {tracked, untracked} = partition_tracked(root, paths)
19+
20+ tracked_diffs =
21+ case tracked do
22+ [] -> %{}
23+ list -> list |> run_diff(root, base) |> parse_unified_diff()
24+ end
25+
26+ untracked_diffs = Map.new(untracked, fn path -> {path, untracked_as_addition(root, path)} end)
27+
28+ Map.merge(tracked_diffs, untracked_diffs)
29+ end
30+
31+ defp run_diff(paths, root, base) do
32+ args = ["-C", root, "diff", "--no-color", base, "--"] ++ paths
33+ {output, _exit_code} = System.cmd("git", args, stderr_to_stdout: true)
34+ output
35+ end
36+
37+ defp partition_tracked(root, paths) do
38+ Enum.split_with(paths, fn path -> tracked?(root, path) end)
39+ end
40+
41+ defp tracked?(root, path) do
42+ {_out, status} =
43+ System.cmd("git", ["-C", root, "ls-files", "--error-unmatch", path], stderr_to_stdout: true)
44+
45+ status == 0
46+ end
47+
48+ defp untracked_as_addition(root, path) do
49+ case File.read(Path.join(root, path)) do
50+ {:ok, content} ->
51+ header = [{:file_header, "diff --git a/#{path} b/#{path}"}, {:file_header, "new file"}]
52+
53+ body =
54+ content
55+ |> String.split("\n")
56+ |> Enum.map(&{:add, "+" <> &1})
57+
58+ header ++ body
59+
60+ {:error, _} ->
61+ []
62+ end
63+ end
64+
65+ defp parse_unified_diff(""), do: %{}
66+
67+ defp parse_unified_diff(text) do
68+ text
69+ |> String.split("\n")
70+ |> Enum.reduce({nil, %{}, []}, &consume_line/2)
71+ |> finalize()
72+ end
73+
74+ defp consume_line("diff --git a/" <> rest = line, {current, acc, lines}) do
75+ acc = stash_current(current, acc, lines)
76+ new_path = rest |> String.split(" ", parts: 2) |> List.first()
77+ {new_path, acc, [{:file_header, line}]}
78+ end
79+
80+ defp consume_line("@@ " <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}) do
81+ {current, acc, [{:hunk_header, line} | lines]}
82+ end
83+
84+ defp consume_line("+++" <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
85+ do: {current, acc, [{:file_header, line} | lines]}
86+
87+ defp consume_line("---" <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
88+ do: {current, acc, [{:file_header, line} | lines]}
89+
90+ defp consume_line("+" <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
91+ do: {current, acc, [{:add, line} | lines]}
92+
93+ defp consume_line("-" <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
94+ do: {current, acc, [{:del, line} | lines]}
95+
96+ defp consume_line("\\ " <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
97+ do: {current, acc, [{:ctx, line} | lines]}
98+
99+ # git metadata lines that appear between "diff --git" and the first hunk
100+ # (or between hunks for binary/empty files). Classify as file_header so
101+ # the renderer can filter them out the same way it filters the diff
102+ # banner lines.
103+ defp consume_line("index " <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
104+ do: {current, acc, [{:file_header, line} | lines]}
105+
106+ defp consume_line("new file " <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
107+ do: {current, acc, [{:file_header, line} | lines]}
108+
109+ defp consume_line("deleted file " <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
110+ do: {current, acc, [{:file_header, line} | lines]}
111+
112+ defp consume_line("old mode " <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
113+ do: {current, acc, [{:file_header, line} | lines]}
114+
115+ defp consume_line("new mode " <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
116+ do: {current, acc, [{:file_header, line} | lines]}
117+
118+ defp consume_line("similarity index " <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
119+ do: {current, acc, [{:file_header, line} | lines]}
120+
121+ defp consume_line("rename " <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
122+ do: {current, acc, [{:file_header, line} | lines]}
123+
124+ defp consume_line("copy " <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
125+ do: {current, acc, [{:file_header, line} | lines]}
126+
127+ defp consume_line("Binary files " <> _ = line, {current, acc, lines}),
128+ do: {current, acc, [{:file_header, line} | lines]}
129+
130+ defp consume_line(line, {current, acc, lines}),
131+ do: {current, acc, [{:ctx, line} | lines]}
132+
133+ defp finalize({current, acc, lines}), do: stash_current(current, acc, lines)
134+
135+ defp stash_current(nil, acc, _lines), do: acc
136+ defp stash_current(path, acc, lines), do: Map.put(acc, path, Enum.reverse(lines))
137+end
lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex
@@ -0,0 +1,2440 @@
1+defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
2+ @moduledoc """
3+ Renders a `SpecLedEx.Review.build_view/3` view-model as a self-contained
4+ HTML string. CSS and JS are embedded inline so the artifact has no
5+ network dependency at view time.
6+
7+ This module does no data fetching of its own — feed it the assembled
8+ view-model and it returns iodata.
9+ """
10+
11+ require EEx
12+
13+ # Vendored Prism.js bundle for syntax highlighting in the diff view.
14+ # Read at compile-time so the runtime artifact stays self-contained.
15+ @prism_assets_dir Path.join([__DIR__, "..", "..", "..", "priv", "spec_review_assets"])
16+ |> Path.expand()
17+
18+ @prism_files [
19+ "prism.min.js",
20+ "prism-markup.min.js",
21+ "prism-css.min.js",
22+ "prism-erlang.min.js",
23+ "prism-elixir.min.js",
24+ "prism-json.min.js",
25+ "prism-yaml.min.js",
26+ "prism-markdown.min.js",
27+ "prism-bash.min.js",
28+ "prism-diff.min.js"
29+ ]
30+
31+ for f <- @prism_files do
32+ @external_resource Path.join(@prism_assets_dir, f)
33+ end
34+
35+ @external_resource Path.join(@prism_assets_dir, "prism.css")
36+
37+ @prism_js Enum.map_join(@prism_files, "\n", fn f ->
38+ File.read!(Path.join(@prism_assets_dir, f))
39+ end)
40+
41+ @prism_css File.read!(Path.join(@prism_assets_dir, "prism.css"))
42+
43+ # covers: specled.spec_review.html_artifact
44+ # All CSS and JS are embedded in the document; no <link> or <script src>
45+ # tags reach the network. The artifact renders identically offline.
46+ #
47+ # covers: specled.spec_review.read_only_viewer
48+ # No <form>, <input>, or <button> that posts state. The HTML is a pure
49+ # viewer; reviewer approval lives on the host platform's existing flow.
50+ @spec render(map()) :: iodata()
51+ def render(view) do
52+ [
53+ "<!DOCTYPE html>\n",
54+ page(view, css(), js())
55+ ]
56+ end
57+
58+ EEx.function_from_string(:defp, :page, ~S"""
59+ <html lang="en">
60+ <head>
61+ <meta charset="utf-8">
62+ <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
63+ <title>Spec Review · <%= h(view.meta.head_ref) %></title>
64+ <style><%= prism_css() %></style>
65+ <style><%= css %></style>
66+ </head>
67+ <body>
68+ <header class="page-header">
69+ <div class="page-header-row">
70+ <h1>Spec Review</h1>
71+ <div class="page-header-meta">
72+ <span class="ref"><%= h(view.meta.base_ref) %><span class="ref-sep">…</span><%= h(view.meta.head_ref) %></span>
73+ <span class="generated"><%= h(format_dt(view.meta.generated_at)) %></span>
74+ </div>
75+ </div>
76+ </header>
77+ <div class="layout">
78+ <aside class="toc" aria-label="Table of contents">
79+ <%= render_toc(view) %>
80+ </aside>
81+ <main>
82+ <%= render_triage(view.triage, view.all_findings) %>
83+ <%= render_subjects(view.affected_subjects, view.adrs_by_id) %>
84+ <%= render_decisions_changed(view.decisions_changed) %>
85+ <%= render_unmapped(view.unmapped_changes) %>
86+ </main>
87+ </div>
88+ <footer class="page-footer">
89+ <span>specled_ex · spec.review</span>
90+ </footer>
91+ <script><%= prism_js() %></script>
92+ <script><%= js %></script>
93+ </body>
94+ </html>
95+ """, [:view, :css, :js])
96+
97+ @doc false
98+ def prism_js, do: @prism_js
99+ @doc false
100+ def prism_css, do: @prism_css
101+
102+ defp render_toc(view) do
103+ [
104+ ~S|<nav><ul class="toc-list">|,
105+ toc_item("triage", "Triage", triage_toc_badge(view.triage)),
106+ ~s|<li class="toc-section"><a href="#subjects">Affected subjects (#{view.triage.affected_subject_count})</a>|,
107+ if view.triage.affected_subjects == [] do
108+ ""
109+ else
110+ [
111+ ~S|<ul class="toc-sublist">|,
112+ Enum.map(view.triage.affected_subjects, fn s ->
113+ ~s|<li><a href="#subject-#{slug(s.id)}"><code>#{h(s.id)}</code>#{render_toc_finding_badge(s)}</a></li>|
114+ end),
115+ ~S|</ul>|
116+ ]
117+ end,
118+ ~S|</li>|,
119+ toc_item("decisions-changed", "Decisions changed (#{length(view.decisions_changed)})", ""),
120+ toc_item("misc", "Outside the spec system (#{length(view.unmapped_changes)})", ""),
121+ ~S|</ul></nav>|
122+ ]
123+ end
124+
125+ defp toc_item(anchor, label, badge) do
126+ ~s|<li class="toc-section"><a href="##{anchor}">#{h(label)}#{badge}</a></li>|
127+ end
128+
129+ defp triage_toc_badge(%{clean?: true}), do: ~s| <span class="toc-pip toc-pip-clean">✓</span>|
130+
131+ defp triage_toc_badge(%{findings_count: 0}), do: ""
132+
133+ defp triage_toc_badge(triage) do
134+ cond do
135+ Map.get(triage.by_severity, "error", 0) > 0 ->
136+ ~s| <span class="toc-pip toc-pip-error">#{triage.findings_count}</span>|
137+
138+ Map.get(triage.by_severity, "warning", 0) > 0 ->
139+ ~s| <span class="toc-pip toc-pip-warning">#{triage.findings_count}</span>|
140+
141+ true ->
142+ ~s| <span class="toc-pip toc-pip-info">#{triage.findings_count}</span>|
143+ end
144+ end
145+
146+ defp render_toc_finding_badge(%{findings_count: 0}), do: ""
147+
148+ defp render_toc_finding_badge(%{by_severity: by_sev, findings_count: count}) do
149+ cond do
150+ Map.get(by_sev, "error", 0) > 0 -> ~s| <span class="toc-pip toc-pip-error">#{count}</span>|
151+ Map.get(by_sev, "warning", 0) > 0 -> ~s| <span class="toc-pip toc-pip-warning">#{count}</span>|
152+ true -> ~s| <span class="toc-pip toc-pip-info">#{count}</span>|
153+ end
154+ end
155+
156+ # covers: specled.spec_review.triage_panel
157+ # When clean? is true the panel collapses to a single confirmation row;
158+ # otherwise it summarizes finding count + severity breakdown + affected
159+ # subject count, lists each affected subject with its severity badge
160+ # (anchored to its card), and exposes a full findings list.
161+ defp render_triage(%{clean?: true}, _findings) do
162+ ~S"""
163+ <section id="triage" class="triage triage-clean" aria-label="Triage summary">
164+ <span class="triage-icon" aria-hidden="true">✓</span>
165+ <span class="triage-headline">No findings · no unmapped changes</span>
166+ </section>
167+ """
168+ end
169+
170+ defp render_triage(triage, findings) do
171+ [
172+ ~S"""
173+ <section id="triage" class="triage" aria-label="Triage summary">
174+ <header class="triage-header">
175+ <h2>Triage</h2>
176+ <div class="triage-counts">
177+ """,
178+ render_severity_pill("error", Map.get(triage.by_severity, "error", 0)),
179+ render_severity_pill("warning", Map.get(triage.by_severity, "warning", 0)),
180+ render_severity_pill("info", Map.get(triage.by_severity, "info", 0)),
181+ ~s"""
182+ <span class="pill pill-neutral">#{triage.affected_subject_count} affected subjects</span>
183+ """,
184+ if triage.has_unmapped_changes? do
185+ ~s"""
186+ <span class="pill pill-neutral">unmapped changes present</span>
187+ """
188+ else
189+ ""
190+ end,
191+ ~S"""
192+ </div>
193+ </header>
194+ """,
195+ render_triage_subjects(triage.affected_subjects),
196+ render_findings_list(findings),
197+ ~S"""
198+ </section>
199+ """
200+ ]
201+ end
202+
203+ defp render_triage_subjects([]), do: ""
204+
205+ defp render_triage_subjects(subjects) do
206+ [
207+ ~S|<ul class="triage-subjects">|,
208+ Enum.map(subjects, fn s ->
209+ badge =
210+ cond do
211+ s.findings_count == 0 ->
212+ ~S|<span class="badge badge-clean">clean</span>|
213+
214+ Map.get(s.by_severity, "error", 0) > 0 ->
215+ ~s|<span class="badge badge-error">#{s.findings_count} #{maybe_plural(s.findings_count, "error")}</span>|
216+
217+ Map.get(s.by_severity, "warning", 0) > 0 ->
218+ ~s|<span class="badge badge-warning">#{s.findings_count} #{maybe_plural(s.findings_count, "warning")}</span>|
219+
220+ true ->
221+ ~s|<span class="badge badge-info">#{s.findings_count} #{maybe_plural(s.findings_count, "finding")}</span>|
222+ end
223+
224+ ~s"""
225+ <li>
226+ <a class="triage-subject-link" href="#subject-#{slug(s.id)}">
227+ <code class="subject-id">#{h(s.id)}</code>
228+ #{badge}
229+ </a>
230+ </li>
231+ """
232+ end),
233+ ~S|</ul>|
234+ ]
235+ end
236+
237+ defp maybe_plural(1, word), do: word
238+ defp maybe_plural(_, word), do: word <> "s"
239+
240+ defp render_severity_pill(_severity, 0), do: ""
241+
242+ defp render_severity_pill(severity, count) do
243+ ~s|<span class="pill pill-#{severity}">#{count} #{severity}</span>|
244+ end
245+
246+ defp render_findings_list([]), do: ""
247+
248+ defp render_findings_list(findings) do
249+ [
250+ ~S"""
251+ <details class="findings-list" open>
252+ <summary>All findings</summary>
253+ <ul>
254+ """,
255+ Enum.map(findings, fn f ->
256+ sev = f["severity"] || ""
257+
258+ ~s"""
259+ <li class="finding-item finding-#{h(sev)}">
260+ <span class="finding-severity"#{title_attr(tooltip(:severity, sev))}>#{h(sev)}</span>
261+ <span class="finding-code">#{h(f["code"])}</span>
262+ #{render_subject_link(f["subject_id"])}
263+ <span class="finding-message">#{h(f["message"])}</span>
264+ </li>
265+ """
266+ end),
267+ ~S"""
268+ </ul>
269+ </details>
270+ """
271+ ]
272+ end
273+
274+ defp render_subject_link(nil), do: ""
275+
276+ defp render_subject_link(subject_id) do
277+ ~s|<a class="finding-subject" href="#subject-#{slug(subject_id)}">#{h(subject_id)}</a>|
278+ end
279+
280+ defp render_subjects([], _adrs), do: ""
281+
282+ defp render_subjects(subjects, adrs) do
283+ [
284+ ~s|<section id="subjects" class="subjects" aria-label="Affected subjects">|,
285+ ~s|<h2 class="section-heading">Affected subjects (#{length(subjects)})</h2>|,
286+ Enum.map(subjects, &render_subject(&1, adrs)),
287+ ~S|</section>|
288+ ]
289+ end
290+
291+ defp render_subject(subject, adrs) do
292+ EEx.eval_string(subject_template(),
293+ assigns: [s: subject, slug: slug(subject.id), adrs: adrs],
294+ trim: false
295+ )
296+ end
297+
298+ # covers: specled.spec_review.per_subject_tabs
299+ # Each subject card exposes four tabs (Spec / Code / Coverage / Decisions)
300+ # that pivot from the prose statement to the supporting artifacts.
301+ #
302+ # covers: specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges
303+ # Findings affecting the subject render as a badge on the card header in
304+ # addition to appearing in the top triage panel.
305+ defp subject_template do
306+ ~S"""
307+ <article class="subject" id="subject-<%= assigns[:slug] %>">
308+ <header class="subject-header">
309+ <p class="subject-statement"><%= SpecLedEx.Review.Html.h(assigns[:s].statement) %></p>
310+ <div class="subject-meta-row">
311+ <code class="subject-id"><%= SpecLedEx.Review.Html.h(assigns[:s].id) %></code>
312+ <%= if assigns[:s].file do %>
313+ <span class="subject-file"><%= SpecLedEx.Review.Html.h(assigns[:s].file) %></span>
314+ <% end %>
315+ <%= SpecLedEx.Review.Html.render_subject_change_badge(assigns[:s].spec_changes) %>
316+ <%= SpecLedEx.Review.Html.render_subject_finding_badges(assigns[:s].findings) %>
317+ </div>
318+ </header>
319+ <div class="tabs" role="tablist">
320+ <button class="tab active" role="tab" data-tab="spec-<%= assigns[:slug] %>">Spec</button>
321+ <button class="tab" role="tab" data-tab="code-<%= assigns[:slug] %>">Code (<%= length(assigns[:s].code_changes) %>)</button>
322+ <button class="tab" role="tab" data-tab="coverage-<%= assigns[:slug] %>">Coverage</button>
323+ <button class="tab" role="tab" data-tab="decisions-<%= assigns[:slug] %>">Decisions (<%= length(assigns[:s].decision_refs) %>)</button>
324+ </div>
325+ <div class="tab-panels">
326+ <section class="tab-panel active" id="spec-<%= assigns[:slug] %>" role="tabpanel">
327+ <%= SpecLedEx.Review.Html.render_spec_tab(assigns[:s]) %>
328+ </section>
329+ <section class="tab-panel" id="code-<%= assigns[:slug] %>" role="tabpanel">
330+ <%= SpecLedEx.Review.Html.render_code_tab(assigns[:s]) %>
331+ </section>
332+ <section class="tab-panel" id="coverage-<%= assigns[:slug] %>" role="tabpanel">
333+ <%= SpecLedEx.Review.Html.render_coverage_tab(assigns[:s]) %>
334+ </section>
335+ <section class="tab-panel" id="decisions-<%= assigns[:slug] %>" role="tabpanel">
336+ <%= SpecLedEx.Review.Html.render_decisions_tab(assigns[:s], assigns[:adrs]) %>
337+ </section>
338+ </div>
339+ </article>
340+ """
341+ end
342+
343+ @doc false
344+ def render_subject_change_badge(%{base_existed?: false}),
345+ do:
346+ ~s|<span class="chip chip-new"#{title_attr(tooltip(:change, :new_subject))}>NEW SUBJECT</span>|
347+
348+ def render_subject_change_badge(%{file_changed?: true}),
349+ do:
350+ ~s|<span class="chip chip-modified"#{title_attr(tooltip(:change, :spec_edited))}>SPEC EDITED</span>|
351+
352+ def render_subject_change_badge(_), do: ""
353+
354+ @doc false
355+ def render_subject_finding_badges([]), do: ""
356+
357+ def render_subject_finding_badges(findings) do
358+ by_sev = Enum.group_by(findings, & &1["severity"])
359+
360+ [
361+ Enum.map(["error", "warning", "info"], fn sev ->
362+ case Map.get(by_sev, sev) do
363+ nil -> ""
364+ [] -> ""
365+ list -> ~s|<span class="badge badge-#{sev}">#{length(list)} #{sev}</span>|
366+ end
367+ end)
368+ ]
369+ end
370+
371+ @doc false
372+ def render_spec_tab(s) do
373+ req_status = id_status_lookup(s.spec_changes.requirements)
374+ scenario_status = id_status_lookup(s.spec_changes.scenarios)
375+ base_existed? = Map.get(s.spec_changes, :base_existed?, true)
376+
377+ [
378+ render_spec_change_callout(s.spec_changes, base_existed?),
379+ render_requirements(s.requirements, s.findings, req_status),
380+ render_removed_items("Removed requirements", s.spec_changes.requirements.removed),
381+ render_scenarios(s.scenarios, scenario_status),
382+ render_removed_items("Removed scenarios", s.spec_changes.scenarios.removed),
383+ render_spec_diff(s.spec_diff)
384+ ]
385+ end
386+
387+ defp id_status_lookup(%{added: added, modified: modified}) do
388+ added_ids = Enum.map(added, &item_id/1) |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
389+ modified_ids = Enum.map(modified, &item_id/1) |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
390+
391+ added_ids
392+ |> Map.new(&{&1, :new})
393+ |> Map.merge(Map.new(modified_ids, &{&1, :modified}))
394+ end
395+
396+ defp id_status_lookup(_), do: %{}
397+
398+ defp item_id(item) do
399+ cond do
400+ is_struct(item) -> to_string(Map.get(item, :id) || "")
401+ is_map(item) and Map.has_key?(item, :id) -> to_string(item.id)
402+ is_map(item) and Map.has_key?(item, "id") -> to_string(item["id"])
403+ true -> ""
404+ end
405+ end
406+
407+ defp render_spec_change_callout(_changes, false) do
408+ ~S"""
409+ <div class="spec-change-callout spec-change-new-subject">
410+ <strong>NEW SUBJECT</strong> — this spec file did not exist on the base ref.
411+ </div>
412+ """
413+ end
414+
415+ defp render_spec_change_callout(changes, true) do
416+ counts = [
417+ {length(changes.requirements.added) + length(changes.scenarios.added), "added"},
418+ {length(changes.requirements.modified) + length(changes.scenarios.modified), "modified"},
419+ {length(changes.requirements.removed) + length(changes.scenarios.removed), "removed"}
420+ ]
421+
422+ if Enum.all?(counts, fn {n, _} -> n == 0 end) do
423+ ""
424+ else
425+ summary =
426+ counts
427+ |> Enum.reject(fn {n, _} -> n == 0 end)
428+ |> Enum.map_join(" · ", fn {n, label} -> "#{n} #{label}" end)
429+
430+ ~s"""
431+ <div class="spec-change-callout">
432+ <strong>Changes in this PR:</strong> #{summary}
433+ </div>
434+ """
435+ end
436+ end
437+
438+ defp render_requirements([], _, _), do: ""
439+
440+ defp render_requirements(requirements, findings, status_lookup) do
441+ findings_by_req = group_findings_by_requirement(findings)
442+
443+ [
444+ ~s|<h4 class="tab-heading">Requirements (#{length(requirements)})</h4>|,
445+ ~S|<ul class="requirement-list">|,
446+ Enum.map(requirements, fn req ->
447+ id = field(req, :id) || ""
448+ statement = field(req, :statement) || ""
449+ priority = field(req, :priority) || ""
450+ stability = field(req, :stability) || ""
451+ req_findings = Map.get(findings_by_req, id, [])
452+ status = Map.get(status_lookup, id, :unchanged)
453+
454+ ~s"""
455+ <li class="requirement requirement-#{status}">
456+ <div class="requirement-header">
457+ #{render_change_chip(status)}
458+ <code class="requirement-id">#{h(id)}</code>
459+ <span class="pill pill-priority pill-priority-#{h(priority)}"#{title_attr(tooltip(:priority, priority))}>#{h(priority)}</span>
460+ <span class="pill pill-neutral"#{title_attr(tooltip(:stability, stability))}>#{h(stability)}</span>
461+ #{render_requirement_finding_badge(req_findings)}
462+ </div>
463+ <p class="requirement-statement">#{h(statement)}</p>
464+ </li>
465+ """
466+ end),
467+ ~S|</ul>|
468+ ]
469+ end
470+
471+ defp render_change_chip(:new),
472+ do: ~s|<span class="chip chip-new"#{title_attr(tooltip(:change, :new))}>NEW</span>|
473+
474+ defp render_change_chip(:modified),
475+ do: ~s|<span class="chip chip-modified"#{title_attr(tooltip(:change, :modified))}>MODIFIED</span>|
476+
477+ defp render_change_chip(_), do: ""
478+
479+ defp render_removed_items(_label, []), do: ""
480+
481+ defp render_removed_items(label, items) do
482+ [
483+ ~s|<h4 class="tab-heading tab-heading-removed">#{h(label)} (#{length(items)})</h4>|,
484+ ~S|<ul class="removed-list">|,
485+ Enum.map(items, fn item ->
486+ id = item_id(item)
487+ statement = field(item, :statement) || field(item, :id) || ""
488+
489+ ~s"""
490+ <li class="removed-item">
491+ <div class="removed-header">
492+ <span class="chip chip-removed"#{title_attr(tooltip(:change, :removed))}>REMOVED</span>
493+ <code class="requirement-id">#{h(id)}</code>
494+ </div>
495+ <p class="removed-statement">#{h(statement)}</p>
496+ </li>
497+ """
498+ end),
499+ ~S|</ul>|
500+ ]
501+ end
502+
503+ defp render_requirement_finding_badge([]), do: ""
504+
505+ defp render_requirement_finding_badge(findings) do
506+ severities = findings |> Enum.map(& &1["severity"]) |> Enum.uniq()
507+ most_severe = Enum.find(["error", "warning", "info"], &(&1 in severities)) || "warning"
508+ ~s|<span class="badge badge-#{most_severe}">#{length(findings)} finding</span>|
509+ end
510+
511+ defp group_findings_by_requirement(findings) do
512+ Enum.reduce(findings, %{}, fn f, acc ->
513+ message = f["message"] || ""
514+
515+ case Regex.run(~r/[a-z0-9][a-z0-9._-]+/, message) do
516+ [token] -> Map.update(acc, token, [f], &[f | &1])
517+ _ -> acc
518+ end
519+ end)
520+ end
521+
522+ defp render_scenarios([], _), do: ""
523+
524+ defp render_scenarios(scenarios, status_lookup) do
525+ [
526+ ~s|<h4 class="tab-heading">Scenarios (#{length(scenarios)})</h4>|,
527+ ~S|<ul class="scenario-list">|,
528+ Enum.map(scenarios, fn sc ->
529+ id = field(sc, :id) || ""
530+ given = field(sc, :given) || []
531+ when_ = field(sc, :when) || []
532+ then_ = field(sc, :then) || []
533+ covers = field(sc, :covers) || []
534+ status = Map.get(status_lookup, id, :unchanged)
535+
536+ ~s"""
537+ <li class="scenario scenario-#{status}">
538+ <div class="scenario-header">
539+ #{render_change_chip(status)}
540+ <code class="scenario-id">#{h(id)}</code>
541+ #{Enum.map_join(covers, " ", fn c -> ~s|<span class="pill pill-cover">#{h(c)}</span>| end)}
542+ </div>
543+ <div class="gherkin">
544+ #{render_gherkin_section("given", given)}
545+ #{render_gherkin_section("when", when_)}
546+ #{render_gherkin_section("then", then_)}
547+ </div>
548+ </li>
549+ """
550+ end),
551+ ~S|</ul>|
552+ ]
553+ end
554+
555+ defp render_gherkin_section(_, []), do: ""
556+
557+ defp render_gherkin_section(label, items) do
558+ ~s"""
559+ <div class="gherkin-row">
560+ <span class="gherkin-label">#{h(label)}</span>
561+ <ul class="gherkin-items">
562+ #{Enum.map_join(items, "\n", fn item -> ~s| <li>#{h(item)}</li>| end)}
563+ </ul>
564+ </div>
565+ """
566+ end
567+
568+ defp render_spec_diff(nil), do: ""
569+
570+ defp render_spec_diff(%{file: file, lines: lines}) do
571+ [
572+ ~s|<h4 class="tab-heading">Spec file changes</h4>|,
573+ ~s|<div class="filename"><code>#{h(file)}</code></div>|,
574+ render_diff_block(lines, language_for(file))
575+ ]
576+ end
577+
578+ @doc false
579+ def render_code_tab(%{code_changes: []}) do
580+ ~S|<p class="empty-tab">No code files in this change set map to this subject.</p>|
581+ end
582+
583+ def render_code_tab(%{code_changes: changes, id: id}) do
584+ anchor_prefix = "code-" <> slug(id)
585+ paths = Enum.map(changes, & &1.file)
586+ tree = build_path_tree(paths)
587+
588+ diff_blocks =
589+ Enum.map(changes, fn %{file: file, lines: lines} ->
590+ anchor = anchor_prefix <> "-" <> slug(file)
591+
592+ ~s"""
593+ <details class="code-change" id="#{anchor}" open>
594+ <summary class="filename"><code>#{h(file)}</code></summary>
595+ #{IO.iodata_to_binary(render_diff_block(lines, language_for(file)))}
596+ </details>
597+ """
598+ end)
599+
600+ render_files_section(tree, anchor_prefix, "Files (#{length(paths)})", diff_blocks)
601+ end
602+
603+ @doc false
604+ def render_coverage_tab(s) do
605+ [
606+ render_coverage_help(),
607+ render_requirements_coverage(s.requirements, s.claims_by_req),
608+ render_bindings_section(s.bindings),
609+ render_verification_section(s.verification)
610+ ]
611+ end
612+
613+ defp render_coverage_help do
614+ ~S"""
615+ <details class="coverage-help">
616+ <summary class="coverage-help-summary"><span aria-hidden="true">ℹ</span> How is requirement coverage computed?</summary>
617+ <div class="coverage-help-body">
618+ <p>Each requirement is verified by one or more <code>spec-verification</code> entries that name it in their <code>covers:</code> list. The verifier inspects each entry and reports the <strong>strongest evidence</strong> it could find for the requirement.</p>
619+ <dl class="coverage-help-tiers">
620+ <dt><span class="strength-badge strength-executed">EXECUTED</span></dt>
621+ <dd>A <code>kind: command</code> entry with <code>execute: true</code> ran and exited 0; or a <code>kind: tagged_tests</code> entry executed its matching test successfully. Requires <code>mix spec.check --run-commands</code> (or <code>mix spec.review --run-commands</code>).</dd>
622+ <dt><span class="strength-badge strength-linked">LINKED</span></dt>
623+ <dd>For a <em>file-kind</em> verification (<code>source_file</code>, <code>test_file</code>, <code>guide_file</code>, …) the target file exists <em>and</em> its content references the requirement id (typically a <code># covers:</code> comment or a literal mention). For <code>tagged_tests</code>, a test in the suite carries <code>@tag spec: "&lt;requirement_id&gt;"</code>.</dd>
624+ <dt><span class="strength-badge strength-claimed">CLAIMED</span></dt>
625+ <dd>The verification entry names the requirement in its <code>covers:</code> list. A textual claim only — nothing else has been checked yet.</dd>
626+ <dt><span class="strength-badge strength-uncovered">UNCOVERED</span></dt>
627+ <dd>No verification entry covers this requirement at all. Add one to <code>spec-verification</code>.</dd>
628+ </dl>
629+ <p><strong>Tightening the floor.</strong> Pass <code>--min-strength linked</code> (or <code>executed</code>) to <code>mix spec.check</code> to require every claim reach that tier or fail. Set <code>verification_minimum_strength: linked</code> in a subject's <code>spec-meta</code> to bake that floor into the spec itself.</p>
630+ <p><strong>Priority</strong> (<code>must</code> / <code>should</code> / <code>may</code>) is the requirement author's RFC-2119 intent. <strong>Stability</strong> (<code>stable</code> / <code>evolving</code>) communicates whether the wording is settled or still being tightened. Hover any chip on this page for a one-line definition.</p>
631+ </div>
632+ </details>
633+ """
634+ end
635+
636+ defp render_requirements_coverage([], _), do: ""
637+
638+ defp render_requirements_coverage(requirements, claims_by_req) do
639+ rows =
640+ Enum.map(requirements, fn req ->
641+ id = field(req, :id) || ""
642+ statement = field(req, :statement) || ""
643+ priority = field(req, :priority) || ""
644+ claims = Map.get(claims_by_req, id, [])
645+ best_strength = best_claim_strength(claims)
646+ meets_minimum? = Enum.all?(claims, &(&1["meets_minimum"] != false))
647+
648+ ~s"""
649+ <li class="cov-req cov-req-#{best_strength}">
650+ <div class="cov-req-header">
651+ <code class="requirement-id">#{h(id)}</code>
652+ <span class="pill pill-priority pill-priority-#{h(priority)}"#{title_attr(tooltip(:priority, priority))}>#{h(priority)}</span>
653+ #{render_strength_badge(best_strength, claims, meets_minimum?)}
654+ </div>
655+ <p class="cov-req-statement">#{h(statement)}</p>
656+ #{render_covering_claims(claims)}
657+ </li>
658+ """
659+ end)
660+
661+ [
662+ ~s|<h4 class="tab-heading">Requirement coverage (#{length(requirements)})</h4>|,
663+ render_strength_legend(),
664+ ~S|<ul class="cov-req-list">|,
665+ rows,
666+ ~S|</ul>|
667+ ]
668+ end
669+
670+ defp best_claim_strength([]), do: :uncovered
671+
672+ defp best_claim_strength(claims) do
673+ strengths = Enum.map(claims, & &1["strength"])
674+
675+ cond do
676+ "executed" in strengths -> :executed
677+ "linked" in strengths -> :linked
678+ "claimed" in strengths -> :claimed
679+ true -> :uncovered
680+ end
681+ end
682+
683+ defp render_strength_badge(:uncovered, _claims, _meets) do
684+ ~s|<span class="strength-badge strength-uncovered"#{title_attr(tooltip(:strength, "uncovered"))}>UNCOVERED</span>|
685+ end
686+
687+ defp render_strength_badge(strength, claims, meets) do
688+ str = to_string(strength)
689+ label = String.upcase(str)
