16+//# sourceMappingURL=/sm/1f55244a569fc0911044b5e4725a97872b6d85b0662a3429a809bb62f713c381.map
017\ No newline at end of file
test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs
@@ -0,0 +1,71 @@
1+defmodule Mix.Tasks.SpecReviewTaskTest do
2+ use SpecLedEx.Case
3+
4+ setup do
5+ Mix.Task.reenable("spec.review")
6+ :ok
7+ end
8+
9+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.html_artifact"
10+ test "writes a self-contained HTML file with no external network references", %{root: root} do
11+ setup_repo(root)
12+
13+ Mix.Tasks.Spec.Review.run(["--root", root, "--base", "main"])
14+
15+ output = Path.join([root, "_build", "spec_review.html"])
16+ assert File.exists?(output)
17+
18+ content = File.read!(output)
19+ assert String.starts_with?(content, "<!DOCTYPE html>")
20+ assert content =~ "<style>"
21+ assert content =~ "<script>"
22+ # The two checks below are what actually enforce self-containment:
23+ # no external stylesheets are linked and no script src= refs are emitted.
24+ # Vendored libraries (Prism) inline their own source which may include
25+ # https:// URLs inside comments — that does not break self-containment.
26+ refute content =~ "<link rel=\"stylesheet\""
27+ refute content =~ "<script src="
28+ end
29+
30+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.same_artifact_local_and_ci"
31+ test "writes to a custom --output path when given", %{root: root} do
32+ setup_repo(root)
33+
34+ custom = Path.join(root, "out/review.html")
35+ Mix.Tasks.Spec.Review.run(["--root", root, "--base", "main", "--output", custom])
36+
37+ assert File.exists?(custom)
38+ refute File.exists?(Path.join([root, "_build", "spec_review.html"]))
39+ end
40+
41+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.diff_against_base"
42+ test "rejects unknown flags", %{root: root} do
43+ setup_repo(root)
44+
45+ assert_raise Mix.Error, ~r/Invalid arguments for spec\.review/, fn ->
46+ Mix.Tasks.Spec.Review.run(["--root", root, "--bogus"])
47+ end
48+ end
49+
50+ defp setup_repo(root) do
51+ init_git_repo(root)
52+
53+ write_subject_spec(
54+ root,
55+ "demo",
56+ meta: %{
57+ "id" => "demo.subject",
58+ "kind" => "module",
59+ "status" => "active",
60+ "summary" => "Demo subject prose statement.",
61+ "surface" => ["lib/demo.ex"]
62+ }
63+ )
64+
65+ write_files(root, %{"lib/demo.ex" => "defmodule Demo do\nend\n"})
66+ commit_all(root, "initial")
67+
68+ write_files(root, %{"lib/demo.ex" => "defmodule Demo do\n def run, do: :ok\nend\n"})
69+ :ok
70+ end
71+end
test/specled_ex/review_test.exs
@@ -0,0 +1,155 @@
1+defmodule SpecLedEx.ReviewTest do
2+ use SpecLedEx.Case
3+
4+ alias SpecLedEx.Review
5+
6+ describe "build_view/3 view-model shape" do
7+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.spec_first_navigation"
8+ test "puts each affected subject's prose statement at the headline of the card", %{root: root} do
9+ setup_repo(root, "auth_subject", "Authentication boundary protects credentials end-to-end.")
10+
11+ change_subject_file(root, "auth_subject")
12+
13+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root)
14+ view = Review.build_view(index, root, base: "main")
15+
16+ assert [subject] = Enum.filter(view.affected_subjects, &(&1.id == "auth.subject"))
17+ assert subject.statement == "Authentication boundary protects credentials end-to-end."
18+ assert subject.title == "Auth Subject"
19+ end
20+
21+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.misc_panel"
22+ test "puts unmapped changed files into unmapped_changes rather than dropping them", %{
23+ root: root
24+ } do
25+ setup_repo(root, "auth_subject", "Auth subject.")
26+
27+ write_files(root, %{"unowned/random.txt" => "hello\n"})
28+ commit_all(root, "add untouched random file")
29+
30+ write_files(root, %{"unowned/random.txt" => "hello world\n"})
31+
32+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root)
33+ view = Review.build_view(index, root, base: "main")
34+
35+ assert Enum.any?(view.unmapped_changes, &(&1.file == "unowned/random.txt"))
36+ end
37+
38+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.triage_panel"
39+ test "marks triage as clean when there are no findings, no unmapped changes, and no affected subjects",
40+ %{root: root} do
41+ setup_repo(root, "auth_subject", "Auth.")
42+
43+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root)
44+ view = Review.build_view(index, root, base: "main")
45+
46+ assert view.triage.clean? == true
47+ assert view.triage.findings_count == 0
48+ assert view.triage.has_unmapped_changes? == false
49+ end
50+
51+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.triage_panel"
52+ test "summarizes findings with severity counts and surfaces them in all_findings", %{root: root} do
53+ setup_repo(root, "auth_subject", "Auth.")
54+ add_unknown_decision_reference(root, "auth_subject")
55+
56+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root)
57+ view = Review.build_view(index, root, base: "main")
58+
59+ assert view.triage.findings_count > 0
60+ assert view.triage.by_severity != %{}
61+ assert length(view.all_findings) == view.triage.findings_count
62+ end
63+
64+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.diff_against_base"
65+ test "uses the explicit --base option when provided", %{root: root} do
66+ setup_repo(root, "auth_subject", "Auth.")
67+
68+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root)
69+ view = Review.build_view(index, root, base: "main")
70+
71+ assert view.meta.base_ref == "main"
72+ end
73+ end
74+
75+ describe "FileDiff.for_files/3" do
76+ test "returns an empty map when no paths are passed", %{root: root} do
77+ assert SpecLedEx.Review.FileDiff.for_files(root, "main", []) == %{}
78+ end
79+
80+ test "parses additions and deletions for a tracked file", %{root: root} do
81+ init_git_repo(root)
82+ write_files(root, %{"foo.txt" => "alpha\nbeta\n"})
83+ commit_all(root, "initial")
84+
85+ write_files(root, %{"foo.txt" => "alpha\ngamma\n"})
86+
87+ result = SpecLedEx.Review.FileDiff.for_files(root, "main", ["foo.txt"])
88+
89+ kinds = result["foo.txt"] |> Enum.map(&elem(&1, 0)) |> Enum.uniq()
90+ assert :hunk_header in kinds
91+ assert :add in kinds
92+ assert :del in kinds
93+ end
94+
95+ test "synthesizes additions for untracked files", %{root: root} do
96+ init_git_repo(root)
97+ commit_initial_empty(root)
98+
99+ write_files(root, %{"new.txt" => "fresh content\n"})
100+
101+ result = SpecLedEx.Review.FileDiff.for_files(root, "main", ["new.txt"])
102+ kinds = result["new.txt"] |> Enum.map(&elem(&1, 0)) |> Enum.uniq()
103+
104+ assert :file_header in kinds
105+ assert :add in kinds
106+ end
107+ end
108+
109+ # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
110+ # helpers
111+ # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
112+
113+ defp setup_repo(root, name, statement) do
114+ init_git_repo(root)
115+
116+ write_subject_spec(
117+ root,
118+ name,
119+ meta: %{
120+ "id" => "auth.subject",
121+ "kind" => "module",
122+ "status" => "active",
123+ "summary" => statement,
124+ "surface" => ["lib/auth.ex"]
125+ }
126+ )
127+
128+ write_files(root, %{"lib/auth.ex" => "defmodule Auth do\nend\n"})
129+ commit_all(root, "initial")
130+ end
131+
132+ defp change_subject_file(root, _name) do
133+ write_files(root, %{"lib/auth.ex" => "defmodule Auth do\n def login, do: :ok\nend\n"})
134+ end
135+
136+ defp add_unknown_decision_reference(root, name) do
137+ write_subject_spec(
138+ root,
139+ name,
140+ meta: %{
141+ "id" => "auth.subject",
142+ "kind" => "module",
143+ "status" => "active",
144+ "summary" => "Auth.",
145+ "surface" => ["lib/auth.ex"],
146+ "decisions" => ["does.not.exist"]
147+ }
148+ )
149+ end
150+
151+ defp commit_initial_empty(root) do
152+ write_files(root, %{".gitkeep" => ""})
153+ commit_all(root, "initial")
154+ end
155+end
0156
ℹ How is requirement coverage computed?

Each requirement is verified by one or more spec-verification entries that name it in their covers: list. The verifier inspects each entry and reports the strongest evidence it could find for the requirement.

EXECUTED
A kind: command entry with execute: true ran and exited 0; or a kind: tagged_tests entry executed its matching test successfully. Requires mix spec.check --run-commands (or mix spec.review --run-commands).
LINKED
For a file-kind verification (source_file, test_file, guide_file, …) the target file exists and its content references the requirement id (typically a # covers: comment or a literal mention). For tagged_tests, a test in the suite carries @tag spec: "<requirement_id>".
CLAIMED
The verification entry names the requirement in its covers: list. A textual claim only — nothing else has been checked yet.
UNCOVERED
No verification entry covers this requirement at all. Add one to spec-verification.

Tightening the floor. Pass --min-strength linked (or executed) to mix spec.check to require every claim reach that tier or fail. Set verification_minimum_strength: linked in a subject's spec-meta to bake that floor into the spec itself.

Priority (must / should / may) is the requirement author's RFC-2119 intent. Stability (stable / evolving) communicates whether the wording is settled or still being tightened. Hover any chip on this page for a one-line definition.

Requirement coverage (10)

EXECUTED › LINKED › CLAIMED › UNCOVERED strongest evidence wins per requirement
  • specled.spec_review.html_artifact must LINKED · 2 claims

    mix spec.review shall produce a self-contained HTML file with all CSS and JavaScript embedded so the artifact renders correctly without any external network resources at view time.

    • command mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs CLAIMED
    • source_file lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex LINKED
  • specled.spec_review.spec_first_navigation must LINKED · 2 claims

    The HTML shall organize content by spec subject as the primary unit, with each affected subject rendered as a card whose headline is the subject's human-readable prose statement rather than its raw YAML or file path.

    • command mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs CLAIMED
    • source_file lib/specled_ex/review.ex LINKED
  • specled.spec_review.per_subject_tabs must LINKED · 2 claims

    Each subject card shall expose four tabs - Spec, Code, Coverage, and Decisions - that pivot from the subject's intent to its supporting artifacts. The Code tab shall organize changes by realized_by binding tier rather than by file path.

    • command mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs CLAIMED
    • source_file lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex LINKED
  • specled.spec_review.triage_panel must LINKED · 2 claims

    The HTML shall render a top-of-page triage panel that summarizes findings (count, severity breakdown) and the affected subject count for the change set. The panel shall collapse to a single confirmation row when there are zero findings, so clean change sets feel clean.

    • command mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs CLAIMED
    • source_file lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex LINKED
  • specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges must LINKED · 2 claims

    Findings affecting a subject shall additionally render as inline badges on that subject's card, in addition to appearing in the top-of-page triage panel.

    • command mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs CLAIMED
    • source_file lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex LINKED
  • specled.spec_review.misc_panel must LINKED · 2 claims

    Files that change in the diff but do not map to any spec subject shall be surfaced in a clearly-labeled "outside the spec system" panel as a flat file diff, rather than excluded from the artifact.

    • command mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs CLAIMED
    • source_file lib/specled_ex/review.ex LINKED
  • specled.spec_review.same_artifact_local_and_ci must LINKED · 2 claims

    mix spec.review shall produce the same HTML artifact locally and in CI, sourced from the same code path with no local-only or CI-only rendering modes.

    • command mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs CLAIMED
    • source_file lib/mix/tasks/spec.review.ex LINKED
  • specled.spec_review.diff_against_base must LINKED · 2 claims

    mix spec.review shall accept an explicit --base ref and otherwise default to the same base detection used by mix spec.next (origin/main, main, master, HEAD).

    • command mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs CLAIMED
    • source_file lib/mix/tasks/spec.review.ex LINKED
  • specled.spec_review.read_only_viewer must LINKED · 2 claims

    The HTML artifact shall be a read-only viewer in v1 with no acceptance state, no per-subject sign-off, and no reviewer-attributed approval recorded by the artifact itself.

    • command mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs CLAIMED
    • source_file lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex LINKED
  • specled.spec_review.gh_pages_distribution should LINKED

    A documented GitHub Actions workflow shall be provided that runs mix spec.review on PR open and synchronize, deploys the rendered HTML to a per-PR path on a GitHub Pages branch, and posts or updates a PR comment containing the link.

    • workflow_file priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex LINKED
Raw spec-verification block (5 entries)

The author's declarations as written in the spec file. The per-requirement view above is computed from this.

  • command mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs executes
    specled.spec_review.html_artifact specled.spec_review.spec_first_navigation specled.spec_review.per_subject_tabs specled.spec_review.triage_panel specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges specled.spec_review.misc_panel specled.spec_review.same_artifact_local_and_ci specled.spec_review.diff_against_base specled.spec_review.read_only_viewer
  • source_file lib/mix/tasks/spec.review.ex declared
    specled.spec_review.diff_against_base specled.spec_review.same_artifact_local_and_ci
  • source_file lib/specled_ex/review.ex declared
    specled.spec_review.spec_first_navigation specled.spec_review.misc_panel
  • source_file lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex declared
    specled.spec_review.html_artifact specled.spec_review.per_subject_tabs specled.spec_review.triage_panel specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges specled.spec_review.read_only_viewer
  • workflow_file priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex declared
    specled.spec_review.gh_pages_distribution

ADRs referenced (1)

NEW specled.decision.spec_review_html_viewer Spec Review Surface Is A Self-Contained HTML Viewer accepted2026-05-03

Context

Specled_ex already produces structured triangulation across spec ↔ binding ↔ test ↔ coverage, but that structure is consumed only by the mix spec.check pass/fail gate. Reviewers see GitHub’s flat, file-first diff view and have no way to read the structure during review. The “no human in the loop” critique against specled_ex (vs. dashboard-first tools like acai) is downstream of that visibility gap — the human IS in the loop on every PR, but they’re reviewing the wrong artifact.

Several distribution shapes were considered for a richer review surface:

  • a CLI text renderer (cheap, no team review)
  • a GitHub-comment markdown summary (limited by markdown’s expressiveness)
  • a custom GitHub App with a check-run UI (requires hosted infra)
  • a standalone web service (richest, biggest investment)
  • a self-contained HTML artifact rendered by a mix task (no hosting, no markdown ceiling, no divergence between local and CI)

We also considered making the artifact interactive — embedding per-subject acceptance checkboxes that produce a signed manifest the reviewer commits back. That couples the viewer to an acceptance state model we have not yet designed.

Decision

mix spec.review produces a self-contained HTML file. CSS and JavaScript are inlined; no network resources are fetched at view time. The same code path renders the artifact locally and in CI.

The HTML is a read-only viewer in v1. It surfaces the triangle (spec / code / coverage / decisions) so a human reviewer can read it, but it does not record acceptance state, per-subject sign-off, or persistent durability of approvals. Acceptance continues to happen on the host platform’s existing flow (e.g., GitHub’s Approve button).

CI distribution uses GitHub Pages: a workflow deploys the rendered HTML to gh-pages under /pr/<PR>/index.html and posts (or updates) a PR comment linking to it. No third-party GitHub App, no hosted backend.

Consequences

The visibility gap is closed at the cheapest point on the spectrum — a single mix task and a small workflow. The artifact is trivially shippable: as a CI upload, attached to a PR comment, archivable, emailable, openable offline.

Choosing read-only for v1 means we ship the viewer first and design acceptance state once we see how reviewers actually use the surface. If we later add per-subject acceptance, the data model will be informed by usage rather than speculation. The cost is that the “human in the loop” critique is only partially answered today: reviewers can see the triangle, but the act of approving a particular subject’s contract is still implicit (one approval covers the whole PR).

GitHub Pages distribution requires the host repo to enable Pages and accept that the rendered artifacts grow in /pr/<PR>/ paths until a separate cleanup job is added. Auto-cleanup of stale paths is intentionally out of scope for v1.

The workflow uses plain git and the gh CLI for the deploy and comment steps, with no dependency on third-party Actions beyond actions/checkout, erlef/setup-beam, and actions/cache. This keeps the supply chain minimal.

.spec/decisions/specled.decision.spec_review_html_viewer.md

Decisions changed (1)

  • specled.decision.spec_review_html_viewer accepted affects: specled.package, specled.mix_tasks, specled.spec_review

Outside the spec system (1)

These files changed but do not map to any spec subject. Triangulation does not apply here — review the diff directly.

Files (1)
  • mix.lock
mix.lock
